title: Named binding on base query
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.base/0` that returns `from(u in User, as: :user)` — a named binding on the base query so downstream composers can use `[user: u]`. Then write `MyApp.Accounts.active/1` that takes the base query and filters active users via `from([user: u] in query, where: u.active == true)`. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Introduces the `as: :user` convention that makes queries reusable across joins; canonical pattern.
research_citation: Curiosum: 'Always defaulting query functions to from(MySchema, as: :my_schema) so functions can be reused across joins'
